arguments.content:
import unittest
import sys
sys.path.append('.')
from src.shopping_cart import calculate_total, process_order, validate_item, InvalidItemError

class TestShoppingCart(unittest.TestCase):

    def test_calculate_total(self):
        # Test with a valid list of items
        items = [{'name': 'Item 1', 'price': 10.0}, {'name': 'Item 2', 'price': 20.0}]
        self.assertEqual(calculate_total(items), 30.0)

        # Test with an empty list
        self.assertEqual(calculate_total([]), 0.0)

        # Test with a list containing an item with a missing price
        items_with_invalid_item = [{'name': 'Item 1', 'price': 10.0}, {'name': 'Item 2'}]
        with self.assertRaises(InvalidItemError):
            calculate_total(items_with_invalid_item)

    def test_process_order(self):
        # Test with a valid order
        order_id = 'ORD-001'
        items = [{'name': 'Item 1', 'price': 10.0}, {'name': 'Item 2', 'price': 20.0}]
        expected_result = {'order_id': order_id, 'total': 30.0, 'status': 'processed'}
        self.assertEqual(process_order(order_id, items), expected_result)

    def test_validate_item(self):
        # Test with a valid item
        self.assertTrue(validate_item({'name': 'Item 1', 'price': 10.0}))

        # Test with an item with a missing price
        self.assertFalse(validate_item({'name': 'Item 1'}))

        # Test with an item with a missing name
        self.assertFalse(validate_item({'price': 10.0}))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    unittest.main()
